name: ExtensionDeviceDispense_Performer
title: R5: Who performed event (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DeviceDispense.performer` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DeviceDispense.performer` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `DeviceDispense.performer` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DeviceDispense.performer` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Who performed the dispense and what they did (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` is part of an existing definition because parent element `DeviceDispense.performer` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
element.requirements:
Allows disambiguation of the types of involvement of different performers. Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` is part of an existing definition because parent element `DeviceDispense.performer` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.function` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Who performed the dispense and what they did
element.definition: Distinguishes the type of performer in the dispense.  For example, date enterer, packager, final checker.

element.comment:

Element `DeviceDispense.performer.actor` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.actor` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
element.requirements:
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.actor` is part of an existing definition because parent element `DeviceDispense.performer` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.actor` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.performer.actor` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: The device, practitioner, etc. who performed the action.  It should be assumed that the actor is the dispenser of the device.
